findings.evidence: Lines 203 and 215 both have the identical shape `Finset.card_le_card (..._subset_all ...).trans (card_all_le_two x y)`, with subset lemmas at `Rectangle.lean:594` and `BlockedRectangle.lean:90`.
findings.fix: Add a general lemma such as `GridRectangleBetween.card_le_two_of_subset_all {s : Finset (GridRectangleBetween x y)} (hs : s ⊆ all x y) : s.card ≤ 2` after `card_all_le_two`, and derive both `card_emptyRectangles_le_two` and `card_fullyBlockedRectangles_le_two` from it.
findings.issue: `card_emptyRectangles_le_two` and `GridDiagram.card_fullyBlockedRectangles_le_two` are two special cases of the same fact: any finite subcollection of `all x y` has cardinal at most two.

summary: The main rectangle count statements are at the right level, but the subset-cardinality corollaries duplicate the same special-case argument instead of exposing the natural general lemma for subcollections of rectangles.
